name: Stromboli Loaves
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup parmesan OR romano cheese -- fine shreds
3/4 cup mozzarella OR provolone cheese -- shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 15x10x1 inch baking pan with foil. Grease the foil; set aside.
2. Divide the dough in half. Roll each half of dough into a 10x8 inch rectangle on a lightly floured surface. Brush 1 tablespoon of the butter over each dough rectangle, leaving a 1-inch plain border. Sprinkle each rectangle evenly with ham, bacon, olives, raisins, and parmesan or romano cheese. Top with mozzarella or provolone cheese and fontina cheese.
3. Carefully fold the edges of the short sides over the filling about 1 or 2 inches; roll up jelly-roll style, beginning with a long side. Moisten the edges and ends with water and pinch to seal. Carefully place the rolls, seam side down, on the prepared baking sheet. Cover and let rise in a warm place until nearly double (about 30 minutes).
4. Combine egg and 1 tablespoon water; brush over loaves.
5. Bake in a 350 degree oven about 30 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack about 30 minutes before slicing. Cut into 1/2 inch slices. Cover and chill any leftovers. Makes about 40 servings.
